subject: LU-6142 llite: use d_is_symlink to test if dentry is a symlink

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Abandoned

On closer inspection, this is broken. If an inode has an IS_DIR() mode, then the d_entry_type is set to DIRECTORY, where there is ->follow_link or not.  And has both directories and files can be foreign-links, this doesn't work.

Restored

Ahhh.. I see what you were getting at now.
15d44e787e17 added code to  ll_splice_alias to set foreign directories to be DCACHE_SYMLINK_TYPE so that d_is_symlink will work on them.
So this patch is good.
I will fix the commit comment.

Patch Set 3:

If you happen to refresh this patch I think the same logic can be applied to ll_splice_alias() in namei.c

Patch Set 3:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

> If you happen to refresh this patch I think the same logic can be applied to ll_splice_alias() in namei.c

I thought so at first too - but no.

The default behaviour of the code behind d_is_symlink() will treat anything that S_ISIDIR() as a directory, whether it has ->get_link or not.  The code is ll_splice_alias is updating the field that d_is_symlink checks in that case to make it look like a symlink.
So before that code, d_is_symlink() won't detect that it is a symlink.  Afterwards it will.

parents.subject: LU-14480 pool: wrong usage with ost list

commit.subject: LU-6142 llite: use d_is_symlink to test if dentry is a symlink
commit.message:
LU-6142 llite: use d_is_symlink to test if dentry is a symlink

Using d_is_symlink() is preferred to testing ->get_link or
->follow_link.

A recent patch made this work for foreign files/dirs by making sure
the entry type in d_flags is correct, so we can simplify the code in
ll_revalidate_dentry().

Fixes: 15d44e787e17 ("LU-12682 llite: fake symlink type of foreign file/dir")
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Ie4c33ae1fb9a660ccbd50e2c70b6cde65cc9b990
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41770
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
